plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate their risk.
Trim SPY to reduce concentration and free up capital.
Increase holdings in QQQI for income generation.
Add XLV for sector diversification.
Trim CRWD to lower tech concentration.
Maintain a higher cash buffer for safety.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly return. Expect lower drawdowns but also reduced growth potential due to reduced leverage and concentration.

plan: Trim SOXL to a minimal position.
Reduce SPY concentration to meet mandate.
Trim QQQ to rebalance index exposure.
Increase CRWD for exposure to quality tech.
Initiate a position in GOOGL based on scanner strength.
Trim SMH to reduce semiconductor concentration.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for risk-first growth, targeting around 2.5% monthly. It blends broad index exposure with quality tech and limited leverage, maintaining moderate risk.

plan: Increase SOXL exposure for aggressive growth.
Trim SPY to reduce broad index concentration.
Add to CRWD, a high-conviction tech name.
Increase SMH exposure to capitalize on semiconductors.
Initiate a position in CRDO from the scanner.
Initiate a position in ANET from the scanner.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets significant growth around 4% monthly by leaning into leverage and concentrated tech/semiconductor plays. Expect amplified gains and substantially higher potential drawdowns, with daily rebalancing risk for leveraged ETFs.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to remove volatility.
Sell high-beta tech like SMH and CRWD.
Significantly increase QQQI for income generation.
Trim SPY to maintain some broad market exposure.
Hold a larger cash buffer for stability.
Focus on steady monthly distributions over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% monthly by prioritizing yield and stability. It significantly reduces risk and volatility by cutting leveraged and high-beta assets, focusing on income-generating ETFs and broad market exposure.
